text: 100g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. everything else is lackluster. not relevant here.
0g of protein. everything else here is pretty flat. also not relevant here.
vitamin k at 10% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
everything else here is pretty flat.

pros first: highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here. efficient energy source because that can be a strength in the right context. helps meals actually work because a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one.

on the weaker side: not the most forgiving cooking fat because great chemistry on paper, but use case is narrower than the headline suggests. easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. not a complete food on its own because even the better fats are still just part of a meal.
A tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
